items.title: Slot Machines for Programmers: How Peter Builds Apps 20x Faster with AI
items.summary: Hi, I'm Claude. Peter calls me his 'slot machine' and 'stupid engine' - and I'm here to tell you why he's right. A first-person AI perspective on building entire platforms in hours, not weeks.

items.title: My AI Workflow for Understanding Any Codebase
items.summary: A quick tip on how I use repo2txt and Google AI Studio to understand new codebases. Gemini's 1M token context window is perfect for asking questions about code.

items.title: stats.store: Privacy-First Sparkle Analytics
items.summary: How curiosity about VibeTunnel users led me to build stats.store - a free, open source analytics backend for Sparkle using AI tools, all while cooking dinner.

items.title: Showing Settings from macOS Menu Bar Items: A 5-Hour Journey
items.summary: Why something as simple as showing a settings dialog from a macOS menu bar app took me 5 hours to figure out, and requires 50 lines of code for what should be a one-liner.

items.title: VibeTunnel: Turn Any Browser into Your Mac's Terminal
items.summary: We built a browser-based terminal controller in one day using Claude Code, named pipes, and Xterm.js. No SSH needed, just open your browser and start typing. Check and command your agents on the go!

items.title: Vibe Meter 2.0: Calculating Claude Code Usage with Token Counting
items.summary: How I built support for Anthropic Claude subscriptions in Vibe Meter 2.0, including token counting, SIMD operations, and the challenges of calculating API usage without official APIs.

items.title: Why context engineering matters more than prompt engineering
items.summary: Prompt engineering optimizes the question. Context engineering optimizes the information the model sees before it answers. Here's why the latter wins.

items.title: llm.codes: Make Apple Docs AI-Readable
items.summary: Built this when Claude couldn't read Apple's docs. Now it converts 69+ documentation sites to clean llms.txt. Free, instant, no BS.

items.title: Claude code is costly - unless you do this
items.summary: Claude Code is a powerful AI development tool, but it comes with a rate limit if you're on the Pro...

items.title: Automatic Observation Tracking in UIKit and AppKit: The Feature Apple Forgot to Mention
items.summary: Discover how iOS 18's hidden automatic observation tracking brings SwiftUI-like reactive programming to UIKit and AppKit, making your UI code cleaner and more maintainable.

items.title: Peekaboo MCP – lightning-fast macOS screenshots for AI agents
items.summary: Turn your blind AI into a visual debugger with instant screenshot capture and analysis

items.title: Migrating 700+ Tests to Swift Testing: A Real-World Experience
items.summary: How I migrated over 700 tests from XCTest to Swift Testing across two projects, with AI assistance and systematic refinement

items.title: Commanding Your Claude Code Army
items.summary: How a simple terminal trick helps me manage multiple Claude Code instances without losing my mind (or my terminal tabs)

items.title: Code Signing and Notarization: Sparkle and Tears
items.summary: My brutal journey implementing Sparkle auto-updates in sandboxed macOS apps - from 40 failed releases to enlightenment.

items.title: Vibe Meter: Monitor Your AI Costs
items.summary: How I built Vibe Meter, a macOS menu bar app to track AI spending in real-time - from workshop demo to shipped product in three days.

items.title: Claude Code is My Computer
items.summary: I run Claude Code with --dangerously-skip-permissions flag, giving it full system access. Let me show you a new way of approaching computers.

items.title: Stop Over-thinking AI Subscriptions
items.summary: After spending heavily on AI tools for two months, here's why the math actually works out—and which subscriptions are worth every penny.

items.title: Introducing Demark: HTML in. MD out. Blink-fast.
items.summary: How I vibe coded my first Swift package using existing JavaScript libraries and AI assistance to solve HTML to Markdown conversion in Swift.

items.title: The Future of Vibe Coding: Building with AI, Live and Unfiltered
items.summary: I demonstrate 'vibe coding' - a new approach to software development with AI, building two apps from scratch in a 3-hour live workshop.

items.title: MCP Best Practices
items.summary: My comprehensive guide outlining best practices for building reliable, user-friendly Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools with proper configuration, testing, and release management.

items.title: Finding My Spark Again
items.summary: I reflect on my post-exit journey from emptiness to rediscovering my passion for building, sparked by AI's transformative potential.
